name: Co-benefits
v0_4_note: As of v0.4 (see docs/methodology-gate-v0.4.md), co_benefits is NOT a scored dimension in the composite. It is attested as an informational 0-100 value and used by assessors to decide whether to set the community_harm disqualifier: a score in the 'None / negative externalities' band (0-9) with documented harm triggers community_harm=true, which caps the grade at BBB. Bands 10-100 are preserved as informational context for buyers who want to filter by SDG alignment, but have no direct effect on the composite. This change encodes the Berg et al. (2025) finding that rewarding co-benefits in a quality rating reinforces the mispricing that quality ratings are supposed to correct.
rationale: Carbon credits can deliver social and environmental benefits beyond climate mitigation. In v0.2-v0.3, these were rewarded as a scored dimension. In v0.4, they function as a harm filter: the dimension's protective role is preserved (bad projects get capped) but its narrative-reward role is removed (good narratives do not inflate the composite).
data_source: SDG certifications, CCB certification, community impact reports (attestation)
on_chain_feasibility: medium (informational only; community_harm flag is the on-chain decision point)

bands.label: Verified High Impact
bands.requirements: SD VISta or Gold Standard SDG certification; >=3 SDGs with quantified, third-party verified metrics; community engagement documented

bands.label: Verified Moderate
bands.requirements: 1-2 SDGs with third-party verified metrics; or CCB Gold/Silver

bands.label: Partially Verified
bands.requirements: Self-reported SDG contributions with some third-party evidence; CCB standard certification

bands.label: Self-Reported
bands.requirements: Project documentation claims SDG contributions without independent verification; reasonable plausibility

bands.label: Minimal
bands.requirements: Limited co-benefit claims; primarily industrial/technological project

bands.label: None
bands.requirements: No co-benefits identified or project has documented negative externalities

notes: This dimension partially compensates for the safeguards gap documented in Carbon Market Watch (2023) -- none of the four major rating agencies incorporate community safeguards.
